Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Robert Moog papers, circa 1965-2005.
Collection Number: 8629

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Robert Moog papers, circa 1965-2005.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8629
Abstract:
Collection contains business documents, correspondence, engineering plans and schematics, audio and video recordings, photographs, instruments and instrument components, and other personal papers documenting his work as an engineer and electronic music pioneer.
Creator:
Moog, Bob (Robert)
Quanitities:
263 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
Access to boxes 58-62 and box 69 restricted until August 30, 2020.
Access to boxes 23-25, 51, and 57, as well as folders 36 and 40-41 of box 58, restricted to the permission of the curator.
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Robert Moog, founder of Moog Music, was an American engineer and pioneer of electronic music, best known as the inventor of the Moog synthesizer.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Robert Moog papers, #8629. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Collection contains business documents, correspondence, engineering plans and schematics, audio and video recordings, photographs, instruments and instrument components, and other personal papers documenting his work as an engineer and electronic music pioneer.
Restrictions on Use:

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.
Restrictions on Use:

Software and digital files stored on obsolete formats are closed to researchers pending digital processing.
Restrictions on Use:

Mold present in all open-reel tapes and videotapes except the following: tr-16422-16426, 16428-16430, 16436-16437, 16439, 16445, 16465-16466, 16468-16470, 16473-16477, 16480, 16486, V-10451-10453, and V-10871. Individuals with weak immune systems may need to take extra precautions.
Restrictions on Use:

Phonograph records in boxes 19, 71, and 109 have been remediated for mold. Consider using nitrile gloves when directly handling.
Restrictions on Use:

Audiovisual media that has been digitized: TR-16146, TR-16347, TR-16377, TR-16509.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Moog, Bob (Robert)
Subjects:
Music and technology.
Synthesizer (Musical instrument).
Synthesizer music.
Musical instruments.
Moog synthesizer.
Keyboard instrument music.
Electronic musical instruments.
Electronic music.
Computer sound processing.
Computer music.
Avant-garde (Music).
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series I. Personal Papers
 1957-2004
Scope and Contents
Includes college notebooks, personal finance, and articles/notes related to personal interests and activities.
Arranged chronologically, except where noted otherwise.
Box 57
Personal finances
 1966-1983
Scope and Contents
Contains bills, check stubs, and bank statements.
Access to box 57 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Box 24
Personal finances
 1983-2004
Scope and Contents
Contains bills, bank statements, taxes, and life insurance/health information.
Access to box 24 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Box 58
Cornell papers
 1957-1965
Scope and Contents
Includes notebooks, assignments, and readings from Moog's years at Cornell. Includes his 1965 thesis as well as his BA diploma from Queen's College.
Lecture notes, notebooks, and assignments arranged by course, as per original order.
Access to folders 36 and 40-41 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Box 59
Personal papers
 1960-2004
Scope and Contents
Contains: articles and notes related to various interests and activities of Moog's including: political organizing with the ACLU; pamphlets from Florence Moog's memorial as well as a selection of her writings; documents pertaining to Moog genealogy; and travel ephemera.
Arranged by subject.
Box 31 Folder 1
Cornell diploma
 1965
Box 31 Folder 2
Columbia diploma
 1957
Box 31 Folder 4
War bond
 1945
Box 111
Florence Moog
 1933-1985
Scope and Contents
Contains notebooks and news clippings, arranged by subject as per original order.
Box 111
Florence Moog
 1933-1985
Scope and Contents
Contains notebooks and news clippings, arranged by subject as per original order.
Box 114 Folder 2
Florence Moog NYU diploma
 1936
Series II. Correspondence
 1942-2005
Scope and Contents
Alphabetical files (boxes 60-61) includes product inquiries, fan mail, catalog requests, invitations, greeting cards, and personal correspondence. Includes handwritten and typewritten letters, faxes, printed emails, photocopies, and carbon copies.
Arranged alphabetically by last name of correspondent (or corporate name where applicable).
Processing Information
Correspondence was removed from envelopes by processing staff. Where letters do not include full dates and/or names, materials were kept in original envelopes.
Box 60
A-M
 1961-2005
Box 61
M-Z
 1961-2005
Scope and Contents
Also includes address lists and correspondence with individuals whose last names could not be determined.
Box 62
Florence Moog
 1942-1987
Scope and Contents
Contains correspondence between Florence Moog and various members of the Moog family (including Robert) as well as correspondence between Florence and friends.
Box 116
Florence Moog
 1959-1984
Scope and Contents
Contains correspondence between Florence Moog and various friends and family members.
Box 69
Moog Family
 1957-1975
Scope and Contents
Contains correspondence between Moog and various family members, primarily Shirleigh Moog.
Arranged by year.
Box 115
Business cards
 1960-2004
Series III. Business Documents
 1954-2011
Scope and Contents
Includes financial records, meeting notes and minutes, phone logs, production notes and planning, press releases, company newsletters, product manuals and advertisements for companies Moog operated or was employed by.
Arranged chronologically.
Processing Information
In order to preserve the context of materials in this series, correspondence related to business-operations and product development was not moved to the alphabetized Correspondence series.
Box 51
Bills and expenses
 1975-1989
Scope and Contents
Contains bills, petty cash receipts, and expenditures (1976-1979, 1982-1989).
Access to box 51 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Box 47
Business records
 1960-1983
Scope and Contents
Contains R.A. Moog Co., Moog muSonics, and Moog Music/Norlin business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production.
Box 48
Business records
 1980-1987
Scope and Contents
Contains Big Briar, Crumar, Synton, and Kurzweil business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production. Also contains 1983-1984 business correspondence.
Processing Information
1983-1984 correspondence was originally organized alphabetically in an accordion file labeled "Business Correspondence." Correspondence was removed from the file , but original alphabetical order has been preserved.
Box 49
Business records
 1987-1998
Scope and Contents
Contains Big Briar, Kurzweil, and Van Koevering business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production.
Box 27
Business records
 1998-2005
Scope and Contents
Contains Big Briar, Van Koevering, and Moog Music business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production. Also contains documentation of trademark disputes regarding the Moog company name.
Box 50
Invoices and bills
 1968-2003
Scope and Contents
Contains invoices and packing slips (1968-1999) and bills/receipts (1990-2003). Invoices for 1983-1984 are filed in the "Business Correspondence" section of box 48.
Box 52
Manuals
 1954-1987
Scope and Contents
Contains owner's manuals, service manuals, and rough drafts of manuals.
Additional drafts of manuals for certain products are present in Series IV.
Box 53
Manuals, brochures and catalogs
 1957-2011
Scope and Contents
Manuals (1985-2000, folders 1-15); catalogs, brochures, advertisements, price lists, and newsletters (1957-2011).
Additional drafts of manuals for certain products are present in Series IV.
Mapcase Folder 3
Quality Works Together advertisement
 1971
Scope and Contents
Poster-sized advertisement for R.A. Moog and Magnetic Recording Systems, Inc (MRS)
Mapcase Folder 29
Wendy Carlos and Keith Emerson Moog advertisement paste ups
 1975
Mapcase Folder 24
Moog promotional posters
 1976-2005
Box 113 Folder 3
Big Briar 100/300 Series Controllers advertisement paste up
 1982
Mapcase Folder 22
Big Briar Moogerfooger banner
 1998
Box 25
Shareholders' agreements, finances, miscellaneous notes
 1967-1973
Scope and Contents
Contains financial records, legal correspondence, stock transfers, and other records documenting the sale of Moog to Waytena and subsequently to Norlin. Also contains undated notes and miscellaneous tax documents dated 1980-1999.
Access to box 25 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Box 23
Taxes and bank statements
 1981-1986
Scope and Contents
Contains check stubs and bank statements (1981-1987) and taxes (1976-1982).
Access to box 23 restricted to the permission of the curator.
Series IV. Schematics, Circuit Notes, and Layouts
Scope and Contents
Includes hand drawn and printed schematics, notes, PCB and panel layouts for circuits developed primarily in the 1960s-1970s. Includes custom designs and modifications for individuals including Wendy Carlos, Keith Emerson, Jan Hammer, Eddie Harris, John Eaton, and Max Brand. Includes associated correspondence with collaborators and customers where applicable.
Arranged alphabetically by project/product name, last name (where project was commissioned or collaborated on), or manufacturer (where files are neither Moog nor Big Briar, i.e.. Maestro, Hammond, Fairlight, etc.). Within each subseries files are arranged chronologically.
Processing Information
Original folders were discarded due to their strong musty odor, though folder titles were transcribed verbatim from Moog's original files.
Processing Information
Items in this series were originally found folded in letter sized folders. Folded items have been removed from their original folders, flattened, and stored in separate oversize folders. In such instances, folders are listed as "Title 1 of 2," "Title 2 of 2," etc. to indicate their original connection.
Processing Information
In some cases loose files were consolidated into project files.
Processing Information
Drafts of product manuals were left in original folders and have not been moved to the manuals series.

Keith Emerson
Scope and Contents
Original folder labeled "Out-of-pocket expenses for Shirley Moog," with notes written on front of folder that read: "1969-70 Performance Synth (drawing of Min A modules in this!); Shipped to Keith Emerson, Sept. 70; Photos of early versions; Documents of instructions and modifications 1970-74."

Series V. Events
 1960-2005
Scope and Contents
Includes flyers, invitations, correspondence, and travel arrangements for various conferences, events, and speaking engagements. Includes printed lectures by Moog where applicable. Events include academic conferences, computer/electronic/experimental music conferences, trade shows, university commencements , workshops, and television appearances.
Arranged chronologically.
Box 64
Awards
 1960-2005
Scope and Contents
Contains documents related to awards given to Moog and related ceremonies.
Box 77
Awards
 1992-2004
Scope and Contents
TEC (1992); Museum of Making Music (undated); MEIEA (1999); NAMM (2004)
Plaques
Box 79
Awards
 1970-1984
Scope and Contents
1970 Billboard Trendsetter award, AES pin, and Svenska Analog-Sympatisorer medal.

 1991

Concerts
 1960-2005

Includes concert performances by Moog and/or Moog associates including Chris Swansen, Lydia Kavina, Herb Deutsch, and Gershon Kingsley.

 1969-1999

Box 63
Conferences, workshops, and speaking engagements

Box 65
Audio Engineering Society (AES) conferences
 1964-2004

Includes conference preprints, ephemera, and advertisements, as well as copies of paper reviews by Moog (with their corresponding papers).

 1976-2003

Contains 1976 poster for an appearance at a high school and 2003 poster for an appearance in Japan.

Series VI. Technical Articles
 1954-1997
Arranged alphabetically by author. Exceptions include subject-based files which were kept intact (i.e.. "Vocoder files" and "Delta modulation").
Box 40
Technical articles I
 1955-1982
Scope and Contents
Includes academic papers, technical articles, and reference materials collected by Moog.
Box 28
Technical articles II
 1955-1982
Scope and Contents
Includes academic papers, technical articles, and reference materials collected by Moog.
EDN circuit blocks
 1975
Scope and Contents
Contains clippings on circuit blocks and engineering from EDN magazine.
Box 30
Moog articles; Electronic Music Review
 1954-1997
Scope and Contents
Includes articles written by Moog, as well as a complete run of the Moog-published Electronic Music Review (1967-1968). Includes 2 reels of Electronic Music Review microfilm. Issues of Contemporary Keyboard Magazine featuring columns by Moog are located in box 42. Speeches delivered by Moog are filed in their corresponding event file in Series V.
Ithaca, New York
Box 36 Folder 5
Soviet electronics handbook
 1957
Box 105
Strobe Tuner Settings for the Historical Scales
 1983
creator: Henderson, Ian H.
Series VII. Clippings
 1920-2005
Scope and Contents
Contains newspaper and magazine clippings of: Moog advertisements, articles about Moog, interviews with/quotes from Moog, and photographs of Moog instruments, collected by both Robert Moog and the Bob Moog Foundation. Also includes articles collected by Moog about electronic music and performers associated with the company, including Wendy Carlos, Lydia Kavina, Clara Rockmore, and Keith Emerson.
Arranged chronologically by decade.
Processing Information
Bookmarks were found in some magazine issues to mark relevant pages. All bookmarks have been left in place.
Box 32
Clippings
 1920-1979
Scope and Contents
Primarily contains clippings from 1960-1979.
Box 54
Clippings

Scope and Contents
Primarily contains clippings from 1970-1989.

Clippings

Clippings

Box 42
Contemporary Keyboard Magazine
 1975-2005
Scope and Contents
Includes several folders of Contemporary Keyboard Magazine (later called Keyboard Magazine), many with bookmarked columns by Bob Moog and Tom Rhea.
Box 31
Van Koevering scrapbook
 1961-1987
Scope and Contents
Scrapbook containing clippings related to David Van Koevering and the Van Koevering family's performances in Curt Emmons Crusaders and other church ensembles.
Box 31 Folder 5
Van Koevering scrapbook materials
 1932-1987
Scope and Contents
Includes photographs and 1932 issue of Radio News with theremin article.
Loose items removed from Van Koevering scrapbook and foldered separately.
Box 101 Folder 4
Old theremin memorabilia - Loudspeaker issues
 1977
Magazines
 1955-2005
Scope and Contents
Publications collected by Moog that do not directly reference Moog or his associates.
Box 56
Magazines
 1955-2004
Scope and Contents
Contains Electronotes (1973-1982); Dr. Dobb's Journal (2003); Sound on Sound (2004); Music Perception (1984-1985); IEEE Spectrum (2003); Keyboard Magazine (2003-2004); Journal of the Acoustical Society of America (1974).
Box 26
Magazines
 1963-2005
Scope and Contents
Contains The New Yorker (1990-1993); The Journal of Music Theory (1963-1969); Wired (2001); Sound on Sound (2002-2003); Attaché (1998); Electronic Musician (2002-2004); Keyboard (2003-2005); Keyboard Magazine (2001); miscellaneous magazines (1995-2005).
Series VIII. Photographs and Slides
Prints and negatives arranged alphabetically within subject subseries. Slides arranged separately.
Processing Information
Photographs and slides were partially processed by the Bob Moog Foundation. Folder titles in this series were devised largely by the Foundation, with some exceptions titled by Moog. A large number of photographs were unlabeled, and as such were given titles by processing staff.
Processing Information
Many of the items in this series were marked with a cataloging number such as "PHO 326" by the Bob Moog Foundation. These cataloging numbers were preserved where possible to reflect the previous custodian's original order.

Series IX. Ephemera
 1970-2005
Box 66
Ephemera
 1970-2005
Arranged by subject.
Scope and Contents
Includes stickers, stationary, blank forms, cartoons and popular culture references to Moog, fan art, Bob Moog business cards, records from the Bob Moog Foundation, and promotional materials for the Moog documentary. Also contains manuals for non-Moog products.
Box 78
Ephemera
 1990-2004
Scope and Contents
Includes scores by Douglas Ovens and Harvey H. Miller, ephemera belonging to Florence Moog, and exhibit posterboards from the Bob Moog Foundation.
Box 79
Florence Moog ephemera
 1910-1984
Scope and Contents
Contains items that belonged to Florence Moog, including a silhouette portrait of Frida Moog.

Series X. Media
 1906-2014
Arranged into subseries by media type. Each subseries arranged alphabetically by artist/creator (or chronologically where creator is unknown). Software arranged chronologically.
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.

Open-Reel Tape
 1954-1996
Conditions Governing Use
Mold present in all open-reel tapes except the following: tr-16422-16426, 16428-16430, 16436-16437, 16439, 16445, 16465-16466, 16468-16470, 16473-16477, 16480, and 16486. Individuals with weak immune systems may need to take extra precautions.
